Output:
from __future__ import annotations

import json
import os
from pathlib import Path
import shutil
import subprocess
from typing import Any
from typing import Optional

from .base import StageResult
from .utils import ensure_stage_dir, stage_artifact_path


def ffmpeg_exists() -> bool:
    return shutil.which("ffmpeg") is not None


def run_ffmpeg(args: list[str], timeout_seconds: int = 1800) -> tuple[bool, str]:
    try:
        completed = subprocess.run(args, capture_output=True, text=True, timeout=timeout_seconds, check=False)
        if completed.returncode == 0:
            return True, ""
        return False, completed.stderr or completed.stdout or f"exit={completed.returncode}"
    except Exception as exc:
        return False, str(exc)


def _render_title_overlay_image(output_path: str, width: int, height: int, lines: list[str]) -> tuple[bool, str]:
    # Deterministic placeholder image artifact for compatibility-only phase.
    payload = f"overlay {width}x{height} {' | '.join(lines)}".encode("utf-8")
    Path(output_path).write_bytes(payload)
    return True, ""


def run_stage8_cover(
    input_video: str,
    output_dir: str,
    task_id: str,
    *,
    forum_post_info: Optional[dict] = None,
) -> StageResult:
    source = Path(input_video)
    stage_dir = ensure_stage_dir(output_dir, task_id)
    if not source.exists():
        return StageResult(success=False, output_file=input_video, error_message="stage8 input missing")

    info = forum_post_info or {}
    raw_cover_titles = info.get("cover_titles") if isinstance(info.get("cover_titles"), list) else []
    cover_titles: list[str] = []
    for item in raw_cover_titles:
        if isinstance(item, dict):
            text = str(item.get("text", "")).strip()
        else:
            text = str(item or "").strip()
        if text:
            cover_titles.append(text)
    if not cover_titles:
        for key in ("cover_title_up", "封面标题上", "cover_title_middle", "封面标题中", "cover_title_down", "封面标题下"):
            text = str(info.get(key, "")).strip()
            if text:
                cover_titles.append(text)

    title_txt = stage_artifact_path(output_dir, task_id, "stage8_cover_title", ".txt")
    title_txt.write_text("\n".join(cover_titles) + ("\n" if cover_titles else ""), encoding="utf-8")
    output_video = stage_artifact_path(output_dir, task_id, "stage8_cover", ".mp4")
    overlay_path = stage_artifact_path(output_dir, task_id, "stage8_cover_overlay", ".png")
    subtitle_for_timing = stage_artifact_path(output_dir, task_id, "stage7_correct_subtitles", ".srt")
    if not subtitle_for_timing.exists():
        subtitle_for_timing = stage_artifact_path(output_dir, task_id, "stage6_5_resub_subtitles", ".srt")

    method = "title_text_only"
    overlay_duration_seconds = 0.0
    fallback_used = False
    strict = os.getenv("CLONECUT_STRICT_EQUIVALENCE", "false").strip().lower() == "true"
    if ffmpeg_exists():
        _render_title_overlay_image(str(overlay_path), 1280, 720, cover_titles)
        overlay_duration_seconds = 1.7 if subtitle_for_timing.exists() else 2.0
        filter_expr = f"[0:v][1:v]overlay=0:0:enable='between(t,0,{overlay_duration_seconds:.3f})'"
        ok, err = run_ffmpeg(
            ["ffmpeg", "-y", "-i", str(source), "-i", str(overlay_path), "-filter_complex", filter_expr, str(output_video)]
        )
        if ok and output_video.exists():
            method = "video_title_overlay"
        else:
            fallback_used = True
            shutil.copyfile(source, output_video)
    else:
        fallback_used = True
        if strict:
            method = "strict_rejected"
        else:
            shutil.copyfile(source, output_video)

    strict_rejected = strict and fallback_used
    payload = {
        "task_id": task_id,
        "cover_titles": cover_titles,
        "cover_title_lines": cover_titles,
        "cover_title_up": str(info.get("cover_title_up", "")),
        "cover_title_middle": str(info.get("cover_title_middle", "")),
        "cover_title_down": str(info.get("cover_title_down", "")),
        "封面标题上": str(info.get("封面标题上", "")),
        "封面标题中": str(info.get("封面标题中", "")),
        "封面标题下": str(info.get("封面标题下", "")),
        "method": method,
        "fallback_used": fallback_used,
        "strict_rejected": strict_rejected,
        "overlay_duration_seconds": overlay_duration_seconds,
    }
    manifest_path = stage_artifact_path(output_dir, task_id, "stage8_cover_manifest", ".json")
    manifest_path.write_text(json.dumps(payload, ensure_ascii=False, indent=2), encoding="utf-8")
    return StageResult(
        success=not strict_rejected,
        output_file=str(output_video if output_video.exists() else source),
        error_message="strict equivalence rejected stage8 fallback" if strict_rejected else None,
        artifacts={"manifest_file": str(manifest_path), "title_file": str(title_txt)},
    )


def run(*args: Any, **kwargs: Any) -> StageResult:
    return run_stage8_cover(*args, **kwargs)

            normalized_result["output_path"] = named_output_path
        return normalized_result

    def _attach_video_render_manifest(self, task: UnifiedTask, result: Dict[str, Any]) -> Dict[str, Any]:
        normalized_result = dict(result or {})
        artifacts = self._collect_video_artifacts(task=task, result=normalized_result)
        if not artifacts:
            return normalized_result

        render_manifest = self._build_video_render_manifest(
            task=task,
            result=normalized_result,
            artifacts=artifacts,
        )
        normalized_result["artifacts"] = artifacts
        normalized_result["render_manifest"] = render_manifest
        return normalized_result

    def _collect_video_artifacts(self, task: UnifiedTask, result: Dict[str, Any]) -> List[Dict[str, Any]]:
        task_dir = Path(self.config.output_dir) / task.task_id
        output_path = str(result.get("output_path") or "").strip()
        named_output_path = str(result.get("named_output_path") or "").strip()
        original_output_path = str(result.get("original_output_path") or "").strip()
        preferred_primary_path = output_path or named_output_path or original_output_path
        primary_resolved = self._safe_resolve_existing_path(preferred_primary_path)

        candidate_files: Dict[str, Path] = {}
        for raw_path in (output_path, named_output_path, original_output_path):
            resolved = self._safe_resolve_existing_path(raw_path)
            if resolved is not None:
                candidate_files[str(resolved)] = resolved

        candidate_dirs: List[Path] = []
        for raw_dir in (
            str(task_dir),
            str(Path(output_path).parent) if output_path else "",
            str(Path(named_output_path).parent) if named_output_path else "",
            str(Path(original_output_path).parent) if original_output_path else "",
        ):
            resolved_dir = self._safe_resolve_existing_dir(raw_dir)
            if resolved_dir is None:
                continue
            if resolved_dir not in candidate_dirs:
                candidate_dirs.append(resolved_dir)

        for base_dir in candidate_dirs:
            try:
                files = sorted(
                    [path for path in base_dir.rglob("*") if path.is_file()],
                    key=lambda item: str(item).lower(),
                )
            except Exception:
                continue
            for path in files:
                if not self._should_expose_video_artifact(path):
                    continue
                resolved = self._safe_resolve_existing_path(str(path))
                if resolved is None:
                    continue
                candidate_files[str(resolved)] = resolved

        artifacts: List[Dict[str, Any]] = []
        used_ids: set[str] = set()
        task_dir_resolved = self._safe_resolve_existing_dir(str(task_dir))
        sorted_files = sorted(
            candidate_files.values(),
            key=lambda item: self._video_artifact_sort_key(
                path=item,
                primary_resolved=primary_resolved,
            ),
        )
        single_artifact_roles = {
            "final_video",
            "video_subtitled",
            "video_no_subtitle",
            "coverless_video",
            "subtitle_srt",
            "subtitle_ass",
            "transcript_text",
            "clip_text",
            "forum_post_info",
            "cover_image",
            "cover_manifest",
            "processing_log",
            "pipeline_state",
            "video",
        }
        selected_files: List[Tuple[Path, str, str]] = []
        seen_roles: set[str] = set()
        for path in sorted_files:
            role, kind = self._classify_video_artifact_role(
                path=path,
                primary_resolved=primary_resolved,
            )
            if role in single_artifact_roles and role in seen_roles:
                continue
            seen_roles.add(role)
            selected_files.append((path, role, kind))

        for index, artifact_info in enumerate(selected_files, start=1):
            path, role, kind = artifact_info
            artifact_id = self._build_video_artifact_id(
                role=role,
                index=index,
                used_ids=used_ids,
            )
            relative_path = self._build_artifact_relative_path(path=path, task_dir=task_dir_resolved)
            stat = path.stat()
            artifacts.append(
                {
                    "artifact_id": artifact_id,
                    "kind": kind,
                    "role": role,
                    "filename": path.name,
                    "path": str(path),
                    "relative_path": relative_path,
                    "size_bytes": int(stat.st_size),
                    "is_primary": primary_resolved is not None and path == primary_resolved,
                }
            )
        return artifacts

    def _build_video_render_manifest(
        self,
        task: UnifiedTask,
        result: Dict[str, Any],
        artifacts: List[Dict[str, Any]],
    ) -> Dict[str, Any]:
        primary_artifact_id = ""
        videos: Dict[str, str] = {}
        subtitles: Dict[str, str] = {}
        texts: Dict[str, str] = {}
        context: Dict[str, str] = {}
        logs: Dict[str, str] = {}
        cover: Dict[str, str] = {}
        pipeline_state: Dict[str, Any] = {}

        for artifact in artifacts:
            artifact_id = str(artifact.get("artifact_id") or "").strip()
            role = str(artifact.get("role") or "").strip()
            path_text = str(artifact.get("path") or "").strip()
            if artifact.get("is_primary") and artifact_id:
                primary_artifact_id = artifact_id

            if role == "final_video" and artifact_id:
                videos["primary"] = artifact_id
            elif role == "video_subtitled" and artifact_id and "subtitled" not in videos:
                videos["subtitled"] = artifact_id
            elif role == "video_no_subtitle" and artifact_id:
                videos["no_subtitle"] = artifact_id

Show input payload 
{
  "command": "@'\nimport json\nfrom pathlib import Path\nimport requests\n\nbase='http://127.0.0.1:18310'\ntid='5d042b64-7417-44a3-887f-b3424b36e504'\nresult = requests.get(f'{base}/api/tasks/{tid}/openclaw-result', timeout=20).json()['openclaw_result']\nart_payload = requests.get(f'{base}/api/tasks/{tid}/artifacts', timeout=20).json()\nartifacts = art_payload.get('artifacts', [])\nroles = {a.get('role'): a for a in artifacts if isinstance(a, dict)}\nrender_manifest = art_payload.get('render_manifest', {})\nchecks = {\n  'executed_mode_is_video_pipeline': result.get('executed_mode') == 'video_pipeline',\n  'openclaw_schema_1_0': result.get('schema_version') == '1.0',\n  'render_manifest_schema_1_0': render_manifest.get('schema_version') == '1.0',\n  'final_video_artifact': 'final_video' in roles,\n  'subtitle_srt_artifact': 'subtitle_srt' in roles,\n  'subtitle_ass_artifact': 'subtitle_ass' in roles,\n  'cover_image_artifact': 'cover_image' in roles,\n  'openclaw_output_clip_text_present': isinstance((result.get('outputs') or {}).get('clip_text'), dict),\n  'state_json_artifact': 'pipeline_state' in roles,\n}\nstate = roles.get('pipeline_state', {})\nstate_path = Path(str(state.get('path') or ''))\nstate_fields = {}\nif state_path.exists():\n  payload = json.loads(state_path.read_text(encoding='utf-8'))\n  state_fields = {\n    'last_completed_step': payload.get('last_completed_step'),\n    'last_completed_step_name': payload.get('last_completed_step_name'),\n    'output_file': payload.get('output_file'),\n  }\nstage_dir = Path(fr'D:\\clonecut\\output\\{tid}\\_stages')\nstage_files = sorted([p.name for p in stage_dir.glob('*') if p.is_file()])\nprint(json.dumps({\n  'task_id': tid,\n  'checks': checks,\n  'artifact_roles': sorted(roles.keys()),\n  'openclaw_outputs_keys': sorted((result.get('outputs') or {
  "command": "@'\nimport sqlite3, json\nfrom pathlib import Path\n\ndb = Path(r'd:\\clonecut\\.worktrees\\smoke-test-mp4\\data\\clonecut.db')\nconn = sqlite3.connect(str(db))\nconn.row_factory = sqlite3.Row\nrow = conn.execute(\"SELECT task_id,status,result_json,metadata_json,output_path FROM tasks WHERE task_id=?\", ('5d042b64-7417-44a3-887f-b3424b36e504',)).fetchone()\nif not row:\n    raise SystemExit('task not found')\nresult = json.loads(row['result_json']) if row['result_json'] else {}\nartifacts = result.get('artifacts') or []\nroles = [a.get('role') for a in artifacts if isinstance(a, dict)]\nrender = result.get('render_manifest') or {}\nprint(json.dumps({\n    'task_id': row['task_id'],\n    'status': row['status'],\n    'output_path': row['output_path'],\n    'executed_mode': result.get('executed_mode'),\n    'artifact_roles': roles,\n    'render_manifest_schema_version': render.get('schema_version'),\n    'render_manifest_subtitles': render.get('subtitles'),\n    'render_manifest_cover': render.get('cover'),\n    'render_manifest_texts': render.get('texts'),\n}, ensure_ascii=False, indent=2))\n'@ | & 'd:\\clonecut\\.venv-p310\\Scripts\\python.exe' -",
  "workdir": "d:\\clonecut\\.worktrees\\smoke-test-mp4",
  "timeout_ms": 120000
}

{
  "command": "$ErrorActionPreference='Stop'\n$root='d:\\clonecut\\.worktrees\\smoke-test-mp4'\n$py='d:\\clonecut\\.venv-p310\\Scripts\\python.exe'\n$runDir=Join-Path $root '.tmp_smoke_runtime2'\n$inputDir=Join-Path $root '.tmp_smoke_input2'\n$outputDir=Join-Path $root '.tmp_smoke_output2'\n$logDir=Join-Path $root '.tmp_smoke_logs2'\nNew-Item -ItemType Directory -Force -Path $runDir,$inputDir,$outputDir,$logDir | Out-Null\n$workerOut=Join-Path $runDir 'worker.out.log'\n$workerErr=Join-Path $runDir 'worker.err.log'\n$apiOut=Join-Path $runDir 'api.out.log'\n$apiErr=Join-Path $runDir 'api.err.log'\n$workerCmd = \"/c set REDIS_NAMESPACE=clonecut_smoke2_a3c2350: && set INPUT_DIR=$inputDir && set OUTPUT_DIR=$outputDir && set LOG_DIR=$logDir && \\\"$py\\\" -m apps.worker.main --host 127.0.0.1 --port 18325 --config config/settings.yaml.example\"\n$apiCmd = \"/c set REDIS_NAMESPACE=clonecut_smoke2_a3c2350: && set INPUT_DIR=$inputDir && set OUTPUT_DIR=$outputDir && set LOG_DIR=$logDir && set CLONECUT_API_START_WORKER=false && \\\"$py\\\" -m apps.api.main --host 127.0.0.1 --port 18320 --config config/settings.yaml.example\"\n$workerProc = Start-Process -FilePath 'cmd.exe' -ArgumentList $workerCmd -WorkingDirectory $root -PassThru -RedirectStandardOutput $workerOut -RedirectStandardError $workerErr\n$apiProc = Start-Process -FilePath 'cmd.exe' -ArgumentList $apiCmd -WorkingDirectory $root -PassThru -RedirectStandardOutput $apiOut -RedirectStandardError $apiErr\nfunction Wait-Health($url, $timeoutSec) {\n  $deadline=(Get-Date).AddSeconds($timeoutSec)\n  while((Get-Date) -lt $deadline){\n    try { $resp=Invoke-RestMethod -Method Get -Uri $url -TimeoutSec 2; if($resp.status -eq 'ok'){ return $true } } catch {}\n    Start-Sleep -Milliseconds 500\n  }\n  return $false\n}\n$workerOk=Wait-Health 'http://127.0.0.1:18325/health' 40\n$apiOk=Wait-Health 'http://127.0.0.1:18320/health' 40\n$pidInfo=[ordered]@{worker_pid=$workerProc.Id; api_pid=$apiProc.Id; worker_health=$workerOk; api_health=$apiOk; runtime_dir=$runDir; output_dir=$outputDir; input_dir=$inputDir; log_dir=$logDir}\n$pidPath=Join-Path $runDir 'pids.json'\n$pidInfo | ConvertTo-Json | Set-Content -LiteralPath $pidPath -Encoding UTF8\n$pidInfo | ConvertTo-Json -Depth 4",
  "workdir": "d:\\clonecut\\.worktrees\\smoke-test-mp4",
  "timeout_ms": 120000
}

已按计划先执行了第 1 项真实烟测，并顺手做了第 2 项首轮退场扫描。

- 隔离 worktree：d:\clonecut\.worktrees\smoke-test-mp4（分支 chore/smoke-test-mp4）
- 烟测任务：task_id=5d042b64-7417-44a3-887f-b3424b36e504
- 证据文件：[openclaw_smoke_result.json](d:/clonecut/.worktrees/smoke-test-mp4/.tmp_smoke_runtime/openclaw_smoke_result.json)

烟测结论
- 通过：
1. API -> worker -> VideoPipeline 全链路跑通（executed_mode=video_pipeline）。
2. openclaw_result.schema_version=1.0。
3. 主成片存在：D:\clonecut\output\test\test_final.mp4。
4. state.json 存在且包含兼容字段：last_completed_step/last_completed_step_name/output_file。

- 未通过（你关心的关键点）：
1. subtitle_srt / subtitle_ass 没进入 artifacts 与 openclaw_result.outputs。
2. cover_image 没进入 artifacts 与 openclaw_result.outputs。
3. render_manifest.subtitles 为空，仅有 cover.manifest。

第 2 项首轮扫描结果（旧主链退场）
- 旧 pre/main/pipeline.py 直接引用还在：
- [internal_pipeline_runner.py:63](d:/clonecut/.worktrees/smoke-test-mp4/apps/worker/internal_pipeline_runner.py:63)
- [internal_pipeline_runner.py:76](d:/clonecut/.worktrees/smoke-test-mp4/apps/worker/internal_pipeline_runner.py:76)
- 但该文件当前未被主路径引用（仅文件自身与测试出现），主视频路径仍在：
- [_process_video_like](d:/clonecut/.worktrees/smoke-test-mp4/apps/worker/service.py:1302)
- 仍有迁移兜底分支 fallback_copy：
- [service.py:1564](d:/clonecut/.worktrees/smoke-test-mp4/apps/worker/service.py:1564)
- [service.py:1613](d:/clonecut/.worktrees/smoke-test-mp4/apps/worker/service.py:1613)
- 由 [settings.yaml.example:53](d:/clonecut/.worktrees/smoke-test-mp4/config/settings.yaml.example:53) 控制（默认 0）。

为什么字幕/封面缺失（已定位）
- artifacts 识别规则目前只认固定文件名 total.srt/total.ass/script.txt：
- [service.py:1980](d:/clonecut/.worktrees/smoke-test-mp4/apps/worker/service.py:1980)
- [service.py:1995](d:/clonecut/.worktrees/smoke-test-mp4/apps/worker/service.py:1995)
- 但实际 stage 产物是 _stage7_correct_subtitles.srt、_stage6_5_resub_subtitles.ass，所以未被收录。
- cover_image 只在 ffmpeg 分支生成 overlay png，fallback 时不会产出图片：
- [stage8_cover.py](d:/clonecut/.worktrees/smoke-test-mp4/domain/video/stages/stage8_cover.py)

如果你同意，我下一步直接进入第 3 项：补齐 artifacts/manifest 契约（字幕与封面收集规则 + 回归测试），然后再复跑一次 test.mp4 验收。

If you call it while in Default mode, it will return an error.\r\n\r\nIn Default mode, strongly prefer making reasonable assumptions and executing the user's request rather than stopping to ask questions.   "truncation_policy": {
    "mode": "tokens",
    "limit": 10000
  }

name: systematic-debugging
description: Use when encountering any bug, test failure, or unexpected behavior, before proposing fixes
---

# Systematic Debugging

## Overview

Random fixes waste time and create new bugs. Quick patches mask underlying issues.

**Core principle:** ALWAYS find root cause before attempting fixes. Symptom fixes are failure.

**Violating the letter of this process is violating the spirit of debugging.**

## The Iron Law

```
NO FIXES WITHOUT ROOT CAUSE INVESTIGATION FIRST
```

If you haven't completed Phase 1, you cannot propose fixes.

## When to Use

Use for ANY technical issue:
- Test failures
- Bugs in production
- Unexpected behavior
- Performance problems
- Build failures
- Integration issues

**Use this ESPECIALLY when:**
- Under time pressure (emergencies make guessing tempting)
- "Just one quick fix" seems obvious
- You've already tried multiple fixes
- Previous fix didn't work
- You don't fully understand the issue

**Don't skip when:**
- Issue seems simple (simple bugs have root causes too)
- You're in a hurry (rushing guarantees rework)
- Manager wants it fixed NOW (systematic is faster than thrashing)

## The Four Phases

You MUST complete each phase before proceeding to the next.

### Phase 1: Root Cause Investigation

**BEFORE attempting ANY fix:**

1. **Read Error Messages Carefully**
   - Don't skip past errors or warnings
   - They often contain the exact solution
   - Read stack traces completely
   - Note line numbers, file paths, error codes

2. **Reproduce Consistently**
   - Can you trigger it reliably?
   - What are the exact steps?
   - Does it happen every time?
   - If not reproducible 鈫?gather more data, don't guess

3. **Check Recent Changes**
   - What changed that could cause this?
   - Git diff, recent commits
   - New dependencies, config changes
   - Environmental differences

4. **Gather Evidence in Multi-Component Systems**

   **WHEN system has multiple components (CI 鈫?build 鈫?signing, API 鈫?service 鈫?database):**

   **BEFORE proposing fixes, add diagnostic instrumentation:**
   ```
   For EACH component boundary:
     - Log what data enters component
     - Log what data exits component
     - Verify environment/config propagation
     - Check state at each layer

   Run once to gather evidence showing WHERE it breaks
   THEN analyze evidence to identify failing component
   THEN investigate that specific component
   ```

   **Example (multi-layer system):**
   ```bash
   # Layer 1: Workflow
   echo "=== Secrets available in workflow: ==="
   echo "IDENTITY: ${IDENTITY:+SET}${IDENTITY:-UNSET}"

   # Layer 2: Build script
   echo "=== Env vars in build script: ==="
   env | grep IDENTITY || echo "IDENTITY not in environment"

   # Layer 3: Signing script
   echo "=== Keychain state: ==="
   security list-keychains
   security find-identity -v

   # Layer 4: Actual signing
   codesign --sign "$IDENTITY" --verbose=4 "$APP"
   ```

   **This reveals:** Which layer fails (secrets 鈫?workflow 鉁? workflow 鈫?build 鉁?

5. **Trace Data Flow**

   **WHEN error is deep in call stack:**

   See `root-cause-tracing.md` in this directory for the complete backward tracing technique.

   **Quick version:**
   - Where does bad value originate?
   - What called this with bad value?
   - Keep tracing up until you find the source
   - Fix at source, not at symptom

### Phase 2: Pattern Analysis

**Find the pattern before fixing:**

1. **Find Working Examples**
   - Locate similar working code in same codebase
   - What works that's similar to what's broken?

2. **Compare Against References**
   - If implementing pattern, read reference implementation COMPLETELY
   - Don't skim - read every line
   - Understand the pattern fully before applying

3. **Identify Differences**
   - What's different between working and broken?
   - List every difference, however small
   - Don't assume "that can't matter"

4. **Understand Dependencies**
   - What other components does this need?
   - What settings, config, environment?
   - What assumptions does it make?

### Phase 3: Hypothesis and Testing

**Scientific method:**

1. **Form Single Hypothesis**
   - State clearly: "I think X is the root cause because Y"
   - Write it down
   - Be specific, not vague

2. **Test Minimally**
   - Make the SMALLEST possible change to test hypothesis
   - One variable at a time
   - Don't fix multiple things at once

3. **Verify Before Continuing**
   - Did it work? Yes 鈫?Phase 4
   - Didn't work? Form NEW hypothesis
   - DON'T add more fixes on top

4. **When You Don't Know**
   - Say "I don't understand X"
   - Don't pretend to know
   - Ask for help
   - Research more

### Phase 4: Implementation

**Fix the root cause, not the symptom:**

1. **Create Failing Test Case**
   - Simplest possible reproduction
   - Automated test if possible
   - One-off test script if no framework
   - MUST have before fixing
   - Use the `superpowers:test-driven-development` skill for writing proper failing tests

2. **Implement Single Fix**
   - Address the root cause identified
   - ONE change at a time
   - No "while I'm here" improvements
   - No bundled refactoring

3. **Verify Fix**
   - Test passes now?
   - No other tests broken?
   - Issue actually resolved?

4. **If Fix Doesn't Work**
   - STOP
   - Count: How many fixes have you tried?
   - If < 3: Return to Phase 1, re-analyze with new information
   - **If 鈮?3: STOP and question the architecture (step 5 below)**
   - DON'T attempt Fix #4 without architectural discussion

5. **If 3+ Fixes Failed: Question Architecture**

   **Pattern indicating architectural problem:**
   - Each fix reveals new shared state/coupling/problem in different place
   - Fixes require "massive refactoring" to implement
   - Each fix creates new symptoms elsewhere

   **STOP and question fundamentals:**
   - Is this pattern fundamentally sound?
   - Are we "sticking with it through sheer inertia"?
   - Should we refactor architecture vs. continue fixing symptoms?

   **Discuss with your human partner before attempting more fixes**

   This is NOT a failed hypothesis - this is a wrong architecture.

## Red Flags - STOP and Follow Process

If you catch yourself thinking:
- "Quick fix for now, investigate later"
- "Just try changing X and see if it works"
- "Add multiple changes, run tests"
- "Skip the test, I'll manually verify"
- "It's probably X, let me fix that"
- "I don't fully understand but this might work"
- "Pattern says X but I'll adapt it differently"
- "Here are the main problems: [lists fixes without investigation]"
- Proposing solutions before tracing data flow
- **"One more fix attempt" (when already tried 2+)**
- **Each fix reveals new problem in different place**

**ALL of these mean: STOP. Return to Phase 1.**

**If 3+ fixes failed:** Question the architecture (see Phase 4.5)

## your human partner's Signals You're Doing It Wrong

**Watch for these redirections:**
- "Is that not happening?" - You assumed without verifying
- "Will it show us...?" - You should have added evidence gathering
- "Stop guessing" - You're proposing fixes without understanding
- "Ultrathink this" - Question fundamentals, not just symptoms
- "We're stuck?" (frustrated) - Your approach isn't working

**When you see these:** STOP. Return to Phase 1.

## Common Rationalizations

| Excuse | Reality |
|--------|---------|
| "Issue is simple, don't need process" | Simple issues have root causes too. Process is fast for simple bugs. |
| "Emergency, no time for process" | Systematic debugging is FASTER than guess-and-check thrashing. |
| "Just try this first, then investigate" | First fix sets the pattern. Do it right from the start. |
| "I'll write test after confirming fix works" | Untested fixes don't stick. Test first proves it. |
| "Multiple fixes at once saves time" | Can't isolate what worked. Causes new bugs. |
| "Reference too long, I'll adapt the pattern" | Partial understanding guarantees bugs. Read it completely. |
| "I see the problem, let me fix it" | Seeing symptoms 鈮?understanding root cause. |
| "One more fix attempt" (after 2+ failures) | 3+ failures = architectural problem. Question pattern, don't fix again. |

## Quick Reference

| Phase | Key Activities | Success Criteria |
|-------|---------------|------------------|
| **1. Root Cause** | Read errors, reproduce, check changes, gather evidence | Understand WHAT and WHY |
| **2. Pattern** | Find working examples, compare | Identify differences |
| **3. Hypothesis** | Form theory, test minimally | Confirmed or new hypothesis |
| **4. Implementation** | Create test, fix, verify | Bug resolved, tests pass |

## When Process Reveals "No Root Cause"

If systematic investigation reveals issue is truly environmental, timing-dependent, or external:

1. You've completed the process
2. Document what you investigated
3. Implement appropriate handling (retry, timeout, error message)
4. Add monitoring/logging for future investigation

**But:** 95% of "no root cause" cases are incomplete investigation.

## Supporting Techniques

These techniques are part of systematic debugging and available in this directory:

- **`root-cause-tracing.md`** - Trace bugs backward through call stack to find original trigger
- **`defense-in-depth.md`** - Add validation at multiple layers after finding root cause
- **`condition-based-waiting.md`** - Replace arbitrary timeouts with condition polling

**Related skills:**
- **superpowers:test-driven-development** - For creating failing test case (Phase 4, Step 1)
- **superpowers:verification-before-completion** - Verify fix worked before claiming success

## Real-World Impact

From debugging sessions:
- Systematic approach: 15-30 minutes to fix
- Random fixes approach: 2-3 hours of thrashing
- First-time fix rate: 95% vs 40%
- New bugs introduced: Near zero vs common

name: test-driven-development
description: Use when implementing any feature or bugfix, before writing implementation code
---

# Test-Driven Development (TDD)

## Overview

Write the test first. Watch it fail. Write minimal code to pass.

**Core principle:** If you didn't watch the test fail, you don't know if it tests the right thing.

**Violating the letter of the rules is violating the spirit of the rules.**

## When to Use

**Always:**
- New features
- Bug fixes
- Refactoring
- Behavior changes

**Exceptions (ask your human partner):**
- Throwaway prototypes
- Generated code
- Configuration files

Thinking "skip TDD just this once"? Stop. That's rationalization.

## The Iron Law

```
NO PRODUCTION CODE WITHOUT A FAILING TEST FIRST
```

Write code before the test? Delete it. Start over.

**No exceptions:**
- Don't keep it as "reference"
- Don't "adapt" it while writing tests
- Don't look at it
- Delete means delete

Implement fresh from tests. Period.

## Red-Green-Refactor

```dot
digraph tdd_cycle {
    rankdir=LR;
    red [label="RED\nWrite failing test", shape=box, style=filled, fillcolor="#ffcccc"];
    verify_red [label="Verify fails\ncorrectly", shape=diamond];
    green [label="GREEN\nMinimal code", shape=box, style=filled, fillcolor="#ccffcc"];
    verify_green [label="Verify passes\nAll green", shape=diamond];
    refactor [label="REFACTOR\nClean up", shape=box, style=filled, fillcolor="#ccccff"];
    next [label="Next", shape=ellipse];

    red -> verify_red;
    verify_red -> green [label="yes"];
    verify_red -> red [label="wrong\nfailure"];
    green -> verify_green;
    verify_green -> refactor [label="yes"];
    verify_green -> green [label="no"];
    refactor -> verify_green [label="stay\ngreen"];
    verify_green -> next;
    next -> red;
}
```

### RED - Write Failing Test

Write one minimal test showing what should happen.

<Good>
```typescript
test('retries failed operations 3 times', async () => {
  let attempts = 0;
  const operation = () => {
    attempts++;
    if (attempts < 3) throw new Error('fail');
    return 'success';
  };

  const result = await retryOperation(operation);

  expect(result).toBe('success');
  expect(attempts).toBe(3);
});
```
Clear name, tests real behavior, one thing
</Good>

<Bad>
```typescript
test('retry works', async () => {
  const mock = jest.fn()
    .mockRejectedValueOnce(new Error())
    .mockRejectedValueOnce(new Error())
    .mockResolvedValueOnce('success');
  await retryOperation(mock);
  expect(mock).toHaveBeenCalledTimes(3);
});
```
Vague name, tests mock not code
</Bad>

**Requirements:**
- One behavior
- Clear name
- Real code (no mocks unless unavoidable)

### Verify RED - Watch It Fail

**MANDATORY. Never skip.**

```bash
npm test path/to/test.test.ts
```

Confirm:
- Test fails (not errors)
- Failure message is expected
- Fails because feature missing (not typos)

**Test passes?** You're testing existing behavior. Fix test.

**Test errors?** Fix error, re-run until it fails correctly.

### GREEN - Minimal Code

Write simplest code to pass the test.

<Good>
```typescript
async function retryOperation<T>(fn: () => Promise<T>): Promise<T> {
  for (let i = 0; i < 3; i++) {
    try {
      return await fn();
    } catch (e) {
      if (i === 2) throw e;
    }
  }
  throw new Error('unreachable');
}
```
Just enough to pass
</Good>

<Bad>
```typescript
async function retryOperation<T>(
  fn: () => Promise<T>,
  options?: {
    maxRetries?: number;
    backoff?: 'linear' | 'exponential';
    onRetry?: (attempt: number) => void;
  }
): Promise<T> {
  // YAGNI
}
```
Over-engineered
</Bad>

Don't add features, refactor other code, or "improve" beyond the test.

### Verify GREEN - Watch It Pass

**MANDATORY.**

```bash
npm test path/to/test.test.ts
```

Confirm:
- Test passes
- Other tests still pass
- Output pristine (no errors, warnings)

**Test fails?** Fix code, not test.

**Other tests fail?** Fix now.

### REFACTOR - Clean Up

After green only:
- Remove duplication
- Improve names
- Extract helpers

Keep tests green. Don't add behavior.

### Repeat

Next failing test for next feature.

## Good Tests

| Quality | Good | Bad |
|---------|------|-----|
| **Minimal** | One thing. "and" in name? Split it. | `test('validates email and domain and whitespace')` |
| **Clear** | Name describes behavior | `test('test1')` |
| **Shows intent** | Demonstrates desired API | Obscures what code should do |

## Why Order Matters

**"I'll write tests after to verify it works"**

Tests written after code pass immediately. Passing immediately proves nothing:
- Might test wrong thing
- Might test implementation, not behavior
- Might miss edge cases you forgot
- You never saw it catch the bug

Test-first forces you to see the test fail, proving it actually tests something.

**"I already manually tested all the edge cases"**

Manual testing is ad-hoc. You think you tested everything but:
- No record of what you tested
- Can't re-run when code changes
- Easy to forget cases under pressure
- "It worked when I tried it" 鈮?comprehensive

Automated tests are systematic. They run the same way every time.

**"Deleting X hours of work is wasteful"**

Sunk cost fallacy. The time is already gone. Your choice now:
- Delete and rewrite with TDD (X more hours, high confidence)
- Keep it and add tests after (30 min, low confidence, likely bugs)

The "waste" is keeping code you can't trust. Working code without real tests is technical debt.

**"TDD is dogmatic, being pragmatic means adapting"**

TDD IS pragmatic:
- Finds bugs before commit (faster than debugging after)
- Prevents regressions (tests catch breaks immediately)
- Documents behavior (tests show how to use code)
- Enables refactoring (change freely, tests catch breaks)

"Pragmatic" shortcuts = debugging in production = slower.

**"Tests after achieve the same goals - it's spirit not ritual"**

No. Tests-after answer "What does this do?" Tests-first answer "What should this do?"

Tests-after are biased by your implementation. You test what you built, not what's required. You verify remembered edge cases, not discovered ones.

Tests-first force edge case discovery before implementing. Tests-after verify you remembered everything (you didn't).

30 minutes of tests after 鈮?TDD. You get coverage, lose proof tests work.

## Common Rationalizations

| Excuse | Reality |
|--------|---------|
| "Too simple to test" | Simple code breaks. Test takes 30 seconds. |
| "I'll test after" | Tests passing immediately prove nothing. |
| "Tests after achieve same goals" | Tests-after = "what does this do?" Tests-first = "what should this do?" |
| "Already manually tested" | Ad-hoc 鈮?systematic. No record, can't re-run. |
| "Deleting X hours is wasteful" | Sunk cost fallacy. Keeping unverified code is technical debt. |
| "Keep as reference, write tests first" | You'll adapt it. That's testing after. Delete means delete. |
| "Need to explore first" | Fine. Throw away exploration, start with TDD. |
| "Test hard = design unclear" | Listen to test. Hard to test = hard to use. |
| "TDD will slow me down" | TDD faster than debugging. Pragmatic = test-first. |
| "Manual test faster" | Manual doesn't prove edge cases. You'll re-test every change. |
| "Existing code has no tests" | You're improving it. Add tests for existing code. |

## Red Flags - STOP and Start Over

- Code before test
- Test after implementation
- Test passes immediately
- Can't explain why test failed
- Tests added "later"
- Rationalizing "just this once"
- "I already manually tested it"
- "Tests after achieve the same purpose"
- "It's about spirit not ritual"
- "Keep as reference" or "adapt existing code"
- "Already spent X hours, deleting is wasteful"
- "TDD is dogmatic, I'm being pragmatic"
- "This is different because..."

**All of these mean: Delete code. Start over with TDD.**

## Example: Bug Fix

**Bug:** Empty email accepted

**RED**
```typescript
test('rejects empty email', async () => {
  const result = await submitForm({ email: '' });
  expect(result.error).toBe('Email required');
});
```

**Verify RED**
```bash
$ npm test
FAIL: expected 'Email required', got undefined
```

**GREEN**
```typescript
function submitForm(data: FormData) {
  if (!data.email?.trim()) {
    return { error: 'Email required' };
  }
  // ...
}
```

**Verify GREEN**
```bash
$ npm test
PASS
```

**REFACTOR**
Extract validation for multiple fields if needed.

## Verification Checklist

Before marking work complete:

- [ ] Every new function/method has a test
- [ ] Watched each test fail before implementing
- [ ] Each test failed for expected reason (feature missing, not typo)
- [ ] Wrote minimal code to pass each test
- [ ] All tests pass
- [ ] Output pristine (no errors, warnings)
- [ ] Tests use real code (mocks only if unavoidable)
- [ ] Edge cases and errors covered

Can't check all boxes? You skipped TDD. Start over.

## When Stuck

| Problem | Solution |
|---------|----------|
| Don't know how to test | Write wished-for API. Write assertion first. Ask your human partner. |
| Test too complicated | Design too complicated. Simplify interface. |
| Must mock everything | Code too coupled. Use dependency injection. |
| Test setup huge | Extract helpers. Still complex? Simplify design. |

## Debugging Integration

Bug found? Write failing test reproducing it. Follow TDD cycle. Test proves fix and prevents regression.

Never fix bugs without a test.

## Testing Anti-Patterns

When adding mocks or test utilities, read @testing-anti-patterns.md to avoid common pitfalls:
- Testing mock behavior instead of real behavior
- Adding test-only methods to production classes
- Mocking without understanding dependencies

## Final Rule

```
Production code 鈫?test exists and failed first
Otherwise 鈫?not TDD
```

No exceptions without your human partner's permission.

Output:
from __future__ import annotations

import json
import os
from pathlib import Path
from typing import Any
from typing import Optional

from .base import StageResult
from .utils import ensure_stage_dir, stage_artifact_path


def run_stage5_ai_text(
    input_video: str,
    output_dir: str,
    task_id: str,
    *,
    forum_post_info: Optional[dict] = None,
) -> StageResult:
    source = Path(input_video)
    stage_dir = ensure_stage_dir(output_dir, task_id)
    task_root = Path(output_dir) / task_id
    if not source.exists():
        return StageResult(success=False, output_file=input_video, error_message="stage5 input missing")

    script_path = stage_artifact_path(output_dir, task_id, "stage5_ai_script", ".txt")
    pointer_path = stage_artifact_path(output_dir, task_id, "stage5_script_path", ".txt")
    manifest_path = stage_artifact_path(output_dir, task_id, "stage5_ai_manifest", ".json")
    clip_text_path = task_root / "clip_text.txt"
    script_text = "Script Draft\n\nKey Points:\n- skeleton output\n"
    script_path.write_text(script_text, encoding="utf-8")
    if not clip_text_path.exists():
        clip_text_path.write_text(script_text, encoding="utf-8")
    pointer_path.write_text(str(script_path), encoding="utf-8")
    use_forum_keywords = os.getenv("CLONECUT_STAGE5_USE_FORUM_KEYWORDS", "true").strip().lower() == "true"
    hotwords = []
    raw_hotwords = forum_post_info.get("hotwords", []) if isinstance(forum_post_info, dict) else []
    if use_forum_keywords:
        seen: set[str] = set()
        for item in raw_hotwords:
            text = str(item).strip()
            if not text or text in seen:
                continue
            seen.add(text)
            hotwords.append(text)
    llm_use_factory = os.getenv("CLONECUT_STAGE5_LLM_USE_FACTORY", "false").strip().lower() == "true"
    llm_provider = os.getenv("CLONECUT_STAGE5_LLM_PROVIDER", "rule").strip().lower()
    strict = os.getenv("CLONECUT_STRICT_EQUIVALENCE", "false").strip().lower() == "true"
    engine = "rule_based"
    llm_error = ""
    fallback_used = False
    strict_rejected = False
    if llm_use_factory and llm_provider == "rule":
        engine = "llm_factory:rule"
    elif llm_use_factory and llm_provider != "rule":
        engine = "rule_based"
        llm_error = "missing api_key for provider"
        fallback_used = True
        if strict:
            strict_rejected = True

    manifest_path.write_text(
        json.dumps(
            {
                "task_id": task_id,
                "forum_post_info": forum_post_info or {},
                "engine": engine,
                "keywords": hotwords,
                "use_forum_keywords": use_forum_keywords,
                "forum_keyword_count": len(hotwords),
                "llm_use_factory": llm_use_factory,
                "llm_error": llm_error,
                "fallback_used": fallback_used,
                "strict_rejected": strict_rejected,
            },
            ensure_ascii=False,
            indent=2,
        ),
        encoding="utf-8",
    )
    if strict_rejected:
        return StageResult(
            success=False,
            output_file=str(source),
            error_message="strict equivalence rejected stage5 fallback",
            artifacts={"manifest_file": str(manifest_path)},
        )
    return StageResult(success=True, output_file=str(source), artifacts={"manifest_file": str(manifest_path)})


def run(*args: Any, **kwargs: Any) -> StageResult:
    return run_stage5_ai_text(*args, **kwargs)

    monkeypatch.setattr(video_pipeline_module, "run_stage4_asr", passthrough_stage)

    assert result.get("output_path") == str(final_output)
    assert result.get("executed_mode") == "video_pipeline"

        "user supplied clip text"
    )


def test_attach_video_render_manifest_collects_primary_subtitle_cover_and_state(monkeypatch, tmp_path) -> None:  # type: ignore[no-untyped-def]
    monkeypatch.setenv("CLONECUT_DB_PATH", str(tmp_path / "clonecut_render_manifest.db"))
    cfg = _build_config(tmp_path)
    service = WorkerService(cfg)

    task_dir = Path(cfg.output_dir) / "render-manifest-task"
    task_dir.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)

    final_video = task_dir / "论坛样本_final.mp4"
    final_video.write_bytes(b"final-video")
    no_subtitle_video = task_dir / "论坛样本_智能剪辑无字幕版.mp4"
    no_subtitle_video.write_bytes(b"no-subtitle")
    subtitle_srt = task_dir / "total.srt"
    subtitle_srt.write_text("1\n00:00:00,000 --> 00:00:01,000\nhello\n", encoding="utf-8")
    subtitle_ass = task_dir / "total.ass"
    subtitle_ass.write_text("[Script Info]\n", encoding="utf-8")
    script_text = task_dir / "script.txt"
    script_text.write_text("原始识别文本", encoding="utf-8")
    clip_text = task_dir / "clip_text.txt"
    clip_text.write_text("裁剪文本", encoding="utf-8")
    forum_post_info = task_dir / "forum_post_info.json"
    forum_post_info.write_text('{"thread_id":"123"}', encoding="utf-8")
    cover_image = task_dir / "论坛封面标题.png"
    cover_image.write_bytes(b"png")
    logs_dir = task_dir / "logs"
    logs_dir.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
    process_log = logs_dir / "funclip_20260326_150000.log"
    process_log.write_text("pipeline ok", encoding="utf-8")
    state_file = task_dir / "state.json"
    state_file.write_text(
        json.dumps(
            {
                "last_completed_step": 9,
                "output_file": str(final_video),
                "step_name": "文件整理",
            },
            ensure_ascii=False,
        ),
        encoding="utf-8",
    )

    task = UnifiedTask(
        task_id="render-manifest-task",
        task_type=TaskType.VIDEO,
        source="unit",
        source_path=str(tmp_path / "source.mp4"),
    )
    Path(str(task.source_path)).write_bytes(b"source-video")

    result = service._attach_video_render_manifest(
        task,
        {
            "success": True,
            "request_type": "video",
            "engine": "internal_pipeline",
            "output_path": str(final_video),
        },
    )

    artifacts = result.get("artifacts") or []
    roles = {str(item.get("role")): item for item in artifacts}
    assert "final_video" in roles
    assert roles["final_video"].get("is_primary") is True
    assert "subtitle_srt" in roles
    assert "subtitle_ass" in roles
    assert "clip_text" in roles
    assert "forum_post_info" in roles
    assert "cover_image" in roles
    assert "processing_log" in roles
    assert "pipeline_state" in roles

    render_manifest = result.get("render_manifest") or {}
    assert render_manifest.get("schema_version") == "1.0"
    assert render_manifest.get("primary_artifact_id") == roles["final_video"].get("artifact_id")
    subtitles = render_manifest.get("subtitles") or {}
    assert subtitles.get("srt") == roles["subtitle_srt"].get("artifact_id")
    assert subtitles.get("ass") == roles["subtitle_ass"].get("artifact_id")
    texts = render_manifest.get("texts") or {}
    assert texts.get("clip_text") == roles["clip_text"].get("artifact_id")
    assert texts.get("script") == roles["transcript_text"].get("artifact_id")
    context = render_manifest.get("context") or {}
    assert context.get("forum_post_info") == roles["forum_post_info"].get("artifact_id")
    cover = render_manifest.get("cover") or {}
    assert cover.get("image") == roles["cover_image"].get("artifact_id")
    logs = render_manifest.get("logs") or {}
    assert logs.get("processing") == roles["processing_log"].get("artifact_id")
    pipeline_state = render_manifest.get("pipeline_state") or {}
    assert pipeline_state.get("last_completed_step") == 9


def test_internal_pipeline_context_keeps_only_forum_snapshot_and_clip_text(monkeypatch, tmp_path) -> None:  # type: ignore[no-untyped-def]
    monkeypatch.setenv("CLONECUT_DB_PATH", str(tmp_path / "clonecut_internal_context.db"))
    cfg = _build_config(tmp_path)
    service = WorkerService(cfg)
    task_dir = Path(cfg.output_dir) / "context-task"
    task_dir.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
    source = tmp_path / "context-source.mp4"
    source.write_bytes(b"video-content")
    user_clip_text = tmp_path / "user-clip-text.txt"
    user_clip_text.write_text("user clip text", encoding="utf-8")

    task = UnifiedTask(
        task_id="context-task",
        task_type=TaskType.VIDEO,
        source="unit",
        source_path=str(source),
        payload={
            "clip_text_file": str(user_clip_text),
            "stage5_model_name": "yunyi-codex/gpt-5.4",
            "file_encoding": "utf-8",
            "stage5_use_preprocessor": True,
        },
        metadata={
            "source": "unit",
            "forum_post_info": {"thread_id": "123"},
            "openai_backend_model": "legacy-model-id",
        },
    )

    options = service._prepare_internal_pipeline_context(task=task, task_dir=task_dir)

    assert "forum_post_info_json" in options
    assert "clip_text_file" in options
    assert "model" not in options
    assert "openai_backend_model" not in options
    assert "file_encoding" not in options
    assert "stage5_use_preprocessor" not in options
    assert options.get("clip_text_file") == str(task_dir / "clip_text.txt")
    assert (task_dir / "clip_text.txt").read_text(encoding="utf-8") == "user clip text"


def test_cancel_pending_task(monkeypatch, tmp_path) -> None:  # type: ignore[no-untyped-def]
    monkeypatch.setenv("CLONECUT_DB_PATH", str(tmp_path / "clonecut_cancel.db"))
    cfg = _build_config(tmp_path)
    service = WorkerService(cfg)

    source = tmp_path / "cancel-source.mp4"
    source.write_bytes(b"video")

    ok, task_id, error = service.submit(
        {
            "task_type": "video",
            "source_url": str(source),
            "metadata": {"source": "unit"},
        }
    )
    assert ok
    assert not error

    cancelled, cancel_error = service.cancel_task(task_id)
    assert cancelled
    assert cancel_error == ""

    task = service.get_task(task_id)
    assert isinstance(task, dict)
    assert task.get("status") == TaskStatus.CANCELLED.value


def test_manual_retry_failed_task(monkeypatch, tmp_path) -> None:  # type: ignore[no-untyped-def]
    monkeypatch.setenv("CLONECUT_DB_PATH", str(tmp_path / "clonecut_manual_retry.db"))
    cfg = _build_config(tmp_path)

from __future__ import annotations

import json
from pathlib import Path

from domain.video.stages import stage2_silence, stage7_subtitle, stage8_cover, stage9_organize


def test_stage2_prefers_auto_editor_when_available(monkeypatch, tmp_path) -> None:  # type: ignore[no-untyped-def]
    source = tmp_path / "input.mp4"
    source.write_bytes(b"fake-video")
    out_dir = tmp_path / "out"
    task_id = "v2"

    monkeypatch.setenv("CLONECUT_STAGE2_MODE", "auto")
    monkeypatch.setattr(stage2_silence, "_command_exists", lambda name: name == "auto-editor")
    monkeypatch.setattr(stage2_silence, "ffmpeg_exists", lambda: False)

    captured = {"cmd": []}

    def fake_run_ffmpeg(args, timeout_seconds=1800):  # type: ignore[no-untyped-def]
        captured["cmd"] = list(args)
        out_idx = args.index("--output") + 1
        Path(args[out_idx]).write_bytes(b"auto-editor-output")
        return True, ""

    monkeypatch.setattr(stage2_silence, "run_ffmpeg", fake_run_ffmpeg)

    result = stage2_silence.run(input_video=str(source), output_dir=str(out_dir), task_id=task_id)
    assert result.success
    assert result.output_file
    assert Path(str(result.output_file)).exists()
    assert "--margin" in captured["cmd"]

    report = out_dir / task_id / "_stages" / f"{task_id}_stage2_silence_report.json"
    payload = json.loads(report.read_text(encoding="utf-8"))
    assert payload.get("method") == "auto_editor"


def test_stage2_strict_mode_rejects_copy_fallback(monkeypatch, tmp_path) -> None:  # type: ignore[no-untyped-def]
    source = tmp_path / "input.mp4"
    source.write_bytes(b"fake-video")
    out_dir = tmp_path / "out"
    task_id = "v2_strict"

    monkeypatch.setenv("CLONECUT_STAGE2_MODE", "auto")
    monkeypatch.setenv("CLONECUT_STRICT_EQUIVALENCE", "true")
    monkeypatch.setattr(stage2_silence, "_command_exists", lambda _name: False)
    monkeypatch.setattr(stage2_silence, "ffmpeg_exists", lambda: False)

    result = stage2_silence.run(input_video=str(source), output_dir=str(out_dir), task_id=task_id)
    assert result.success is False
    assert "strict equivalence" in str(result.error_message)

    report = out_dir / task_id / "_stages" / f"{task_id}_stage2_silence_report.json"
    payload = json.loads(report.read_text(encoding="utf-8"))
    assert payload.get("method") == "strict_rejected"
    assert payload.get("fallback_used") is True


def test_stage2_silenceremove_codec_prefers_stage2_env(monkeypatch, tmp_path) -> None:  # type: ignore[no-untyped-def]
    source = tmp_path / "input.mp4"
    target = tmp_path / "out.mp4"
    source.write_bytes(b"fake-video")
    monkeypatch.setenv("CLONECUT_STAGE2_VIDEO_CODEC", "hevc_nvenc")
    monkeypatch.setenv("CLONECUT_VIDEO_CODEC", "libx264")

    captured: list[list[str]] = []

    def fake_run_ffmpeg(args, timeout_seconds=1800):  # type: ignore[no-untyped-def]
        captured.append(list(args))
        return True, ""

    monkeypatch.setattr(stage2_silence, "run_ffmpeg", fake_run_ffmpeg)
    ok, error = stage2_silence._run_ffmpeg_silence_remove(source=source, target=target)  # type: ignore[attr-defined]

    assert ok is True
    assert error == ""
    assert len(captured) == 1
    assert captured[0][captured[0].index("-c:v") + 1] == "hevc_nvenc"


def test_stage2_silenceremove_codec_falls_back_to_global_env(monkeypatch, tmp_path) -> None:  # type: ignore[no-untyped-def]
    source = tmp_path / "input.mp4"
    target = tmp_path / "out.mp4"
    source.write_bytes(b"fake-video")
    monkeypatch.delenv("CLONECUT_STAGE2_VIDEO_CODEC", raising=False)
    monkeypatch.setenv("CLONECUT_VIDEO_CODEC", "h264_nvenc")

    captured: list[list[str]] = []

    def fake_run_ffmpeg(args, timeout_seconds=1800):  # type: ignore[no-untyped-def]
        captured.append(list(args))
        return True, ""

    monkeypatch.setattr(stage2_silence, "run_ffmpeg", fake_run_ffmpeg)
    ok, error = stage2_silence._run_ffmpeg_silence_remove(source=source, target=target)  # type: ignore[attr-defined]

    assert ok is True
    assert error == ""
    assert len(captured) == 1
    assert captured[0][captured[0].index("-c:v") + 1] == "h264_nvenc"


def test_stage2_silenceremove_gpu_codec_failure_downgrades_to_libx264(monkeypatch, tmp_path) -> None:  # type: ignore[no-untyped-def]
    source = tmp_path / "input.mp4"
    target = tmp_path / "out.mp4"
    source.write_bytes(b"fake-video")
    monkeypatch.setenv("CLONECUT_STAGE2_VIDEO_CODEC", "h264_nvenc")

    captured: list[list[str]] = []
    calls = {"count": 0}

    def fake_run_ffmpeg(args, timeout_seconds=1800):  # type: ignore[no-untyped-def]
        captured.append(list(args))
        calls["count"] += 1
        if calls["count"] == 1:
            return False, "No NVENC capable devices found"
        return True, ""

    monkeypatch.setattr(stage2_silence, "run_ffmpeg", fake_run_ffmpeg)
    ok, error = stage2_silence._run_ffmpeg_silence_remove(source=source, target=target)  # type: ignore[attr-defined]

    assert ok is True
    assert error == ""
    assert len(captured) == 2
    codecs = [cmd[cmd.index("-c:v") + 1] for cmd in captured]
    assert codecs == ["h264_nvenc", "libx264"]


def test_stage7_uses_burn_mode_before_mux(monkeypatch, tmp_path) -> None:  # type: ignore[no-untyped-def]
    source = tmp_path / "input.mp4"
    source.write_bytes(b"fake-video")
    out_dir = tmp_path / "out"
    task_id = "v7"
    stage_dir = out_dir / task_id / "_stages"
    stage_dir.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
    subtitle = stage_dir / f"{task_id}_stage6_5_resub_subtitles.srt"
    subtitle.write_text(
        "\n".join(
            [
                "1",
                "00:00:00,000 --> 00:00:01,000",
                "hello",
                "",
            ]
        ),
        encoding="utf-8",
    )

    monkeypatch.setenv("CLONECUT_STAGE7_MODE", "auto")
    monkeypatch.setattr(stage7_subtitle, "ffmpeg_exists", lambda: True)
    captured = {"cmd": []}

    def fake_run_ffmpeg(args, timeout_seconds=1800):  # type: ignore[no-untyped-def]
        captured["cmd"] = list(args)
        Path(args[-1]).write_bytes(b"burned-video")
        return True, ""

    monkeypatch.setattr(stage7_subtitle, "run_ffmpeg", fake_run_ffmpeg)
    result = stage7_subtitle.run(input_video=str(source), output_dir=str(out_dir), task_id=task_id)
    assert result.success
    assert result.output_file
    assert Path(str(result.output_file)).exists()
    assert "-vf" in captured["cmd"]

    manifest = stage_dir / f"{task_id}_stage7_subtitle_manifest.json"
    payload = json.loads(manifest.read_text(encoding="utf-8"))
    assert payload.get("method") == "burn_subtitle"


def test_stage7_strict_mode_rejects_copy_fallback(monkeypatch, tmp_path) -> None:  # type: ignore[no-untyped-def]
    source = tmp_path / "input.mp4"
    source.write_bytes(b"fake-video")
    out_dir = tmp_path / "out"
    task_id = "v7_strict"

    monkeypatch.setenv("CLONECUT_STAGE7_MODE", "auto")
    monkeypatch.setenv("CLONECUT_STRICT_EQUIVALENCE", "true")
    monkeypatch.setattr(stage7_subtitle, "ffmpeg_exists", lambda: False)

    result = stage7_subtitle.run(input_video=str(source), output_dir=str(out_dir), task_id=task_id)
    assert result.success is False
    assert "strict equivalence" in str(result.error_message)

    manifest = out_dir / task_id / "_stages" / f"{task_id}_stage7_subtitle_manifest.json"
    payload = json.loads(manifest.read_text(encoding="utf-8"))
    assert payload.get("method") == "strict_rejected"
    assert payload.get("fallback_used") is True


def test_stage7_prefers_ass_subtitle_input(monkeypatch, tmp_path) -> None:  # type: ignore[no-untyped-def]
    source = tmp_path / "input.mp4"
    source.write_bytes(b"fake-video")
    out_dir = tmp_path / "out"
    task_id = "v7_ass"
    stage_dir = out_dir / task_id / "_stages"
    stage_dir.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
    srt = stage_dir / f"{task_id}_stage6_5_resub_subtitles.srt"
    ass = stage_dir / f"{task_id}_stage6_5_resub_subtitles.ass"
    srt.write_text(
        "\n".join(
            [
                "1",
                "00:00:00,000 --> 00:00:01,000",
                "hello srt",
                "",
            ]
        ),
        encoding="utf-8",
    )
    ass.write_text(
        "\n".join(
            [
                "[Script Info]",
                "Title: ass subtitle",
                "",
                "[Events]",
                "Format: Layer, Start, End, Style, Name, MarginL, MarginR, MarginV, Effect, Text",
                "Dialogue: 0,0:00:00.00,0:00:01.00,Default,,0,0,0,,hello ass",
            ]
        ),
        encoding="utf-8",
    )

    monkeypatch.setenv("CLONECUT_STAGE7_MODE", "burn")
    monkeypatch.setattr(stage7_subtitle, "ffmpeg_exists", lambda: True)
    captured = {"cmd": []}

    def fake_run_ffmpeg(args, timeout_seconds=1800):  # type: ignore[no-untyped-def]
        captured["cmd"] = list(args)
        Path(args[-1]).write_bytes(b"burned-video")
        return True, ""

    monkeypatch.setattr(stage7_subtitle, "run_ffmpeg", fake_run_ffmpeg)
    result = stage7_subtitle.run(input_video=str(source), output_dir=str(out_dir), task_id=task_id)
    assert result.success
    vf_expr = captured["cmd"][captured["cmd"].index("-vf") + 1]
    assert vf_expr.startswith("ass=")


def test_stage7_srt_burn_applies_resolution_adaptive_font_size(monkeypatch, tmp_path) -> None:  # type: ignore[no-untyped-def]
    source = tmp_path / "input.mp4"
    source.write_bytes(b"fake-video")
    out_dir = tmp_path / "out"
    task_id = "v7_font"
    stage_dir = out_dir / task_id / "_stages"
    stage_dir.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
    subtitle = stage_dir / f"{task_id}_stage6_5_resub_subtitles.srt"
    subtitle.write_text(
        "\n".join(
            [
                "1",
                "00:00:00,000 --> 00:00:01,000",
                "hello",
                "",
            ]
        ),
        encoding="utf-8",
    )

    monkeypatch.setenv("CLONECUT_STAGE7_MODE", "burn")
    monkeypatch.setattr(stage7_subtitle, "ffmpeg_exists", lambda: True)
    monkeypatch.setattr(stage7_subtitle, "_probe_video_resolution", lambda _source: (1920, 1080))
    captured = {"cmd": []}

    def fake_run_ffmpeg(args, timeout_seconds=1800):  # type: ignore[no-untyped-def]
        captured["cmd"] = list(args)
        Path(args[-1]).write_bytes(b"burned-video")
        return True, ""

    monkeypatch.setattr(stage7_subtitle, "run_ffmpeg", fake_run_ffmpeg)
    result = stage7_subtitle.run(input_video=str(source), output_dir=str(out_dir), task_id=task_id)
    assert result.success
    vf_expr = captured["cmd"][captured["cmd"].index("-vf") + 1]
    assert "force_style='Fontsize=54'" in vf_expr


def test_stage9_archives_stage_artifacts(monkeypatch, tmp_path) -> None:  # type: ignore[no-untyped-def]
    out_dir = tmp_path / "out"
    task_id = "v9"
    stage_dir = out_dir / task_id / "_stages"
    stage_dir.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
    (stage_dir / "note.txt").write_text("stage artifact", encoding="utf-8")

    source = stage_dir / f"{task_id}_stage8_cover.mp4"
    source.write_bytes(b"final-video")

    result = stage9_organize.run(
        input_video=str(source),
        output_dir=str(out_dir),
        task_id=task_id,
        keep_intermediate_files=True,
    )
    assert result.success
    assert result.output_file

    final_path = out_dir / task_id / f"{task_id}_final.mp4"
    assert final_path.exists()
    legacy_final_path = out_dir / task_id / f"{task_id}.mp4"
    assert legacy_final_path.exists()

    archive_dir = out_dir / task_id / f"{task_id}_artifacts"
    assert archive_dir.exists()
    assert (archive_dir / "_stages" / "note.txt").exists()
    assert (out_dir / task_id / "logs" / "_stages" / "note.txt").exists()

    manifest = stage_dir / f"{task_id}_stage9_organize_manifest.json"
    payload = json.loads(manifest.read_text(encoding="utf-8"))
    assert payload.get("archive_dir")
    assert payload.get("final_output_dir") == str(out_dir / task_id)


def test_stage9_prefers_original_filename_for_final_directory(tmp_path) -> None:  # type: ignore[no-untyped-def]
    out_dir = tmp_path / "out"
    task_id = "v9_forum"
    stage_dir = out_dir / task_id / "_stages"
    stage_dir.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
    source = stage_dir / f"{task_id}_stage8_cover.mp4"
    source.write_bytes(b"final-video")

    result = stage9_organize.run(
        input_video=str(source),
        output_dir=str(out_dir),
        task_id=task_id,
        keep_intermediate_files=True,
        forum_post_info={"original_filename": "论坛样本视频.mp4"},
    )
    assert result.success
    assert result.output_file
    final_path = Path(str(result.output_file))
    assert final_path.name == "论坛样本视频_final.mp4"
    assert final_path.parent.name == "论坛样本视频"


def test_stage8_writes_cover_title_artifacts(monkeypatch, tmp_path) -> None:  # type: ignore[no-untyped-def]
    source = tmp_path / "input.mp4"
    source.write_bytes(b"fake-video")
    out_dir = tmp_path / "out"
    task_id = "v8"

    monkeypatch.setattr(stage8_cover, "ffmpeg_exists", lambda: False)
    result = stage8_cover.run(
        input_video=str(source),
        output_dir=str(out_dir),
        task_id=task_id,
        forum_post_info={
            "cover_titles": [
                {"position": "up", "text": "主标题"},
                {"position": "down", "text": "副标题"},
            ]
        },
    )
    assert result.success
    stage_dir = out_dir / task_id / "_stages"
    title_txt = stage_dir / f"{task_id}_stage8_cover_title.txt"
    manifest = stage_dir / f"{task_id}_stage8_cover_manifest.json"
    assert title_txt.exists()
    assert manifest.exists()
    payload = json.loads(manifest.read_text(encoding="utf-8"))
    assert payload.get("cover_title_lines") == ["主标题", "副标题"]


def test_stage8_applies_video_title_overlay_with_subtitle_timing(monkeypatch, tmp_path) -> None:  # type: ignore[no-untyped-def]
    source = tmp_path / "input.mp4"
    source.write_bytes(b"fake-video")
    out_dir = tmp_path / "out"
    task_id = "v8_overlay"
    stage_dir = out_dir / task_id / "_stages"
    stage_dir.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
    subtitle = stage_dir / f"{task_id}_stage7_correct_subtitles.srt"
    subtitle.write_text(
        "\n".join(
            [
                "1",
                "00:00:02,000 --> 00:00:04,000",
                "first subtitle",
                "",
            ]
        ),
        encoding="utf-8",
    )

    monkeypatch.setattr(stage8_cover, "ffmpeg_exists", lambda: True)
    captured = {"cmds": []}

    def fake_render_overlay(output_path, width, height, lines):  # type: ignore[no-untyped-def]
        Path(output_path).parent.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
        Path(output_path).write_bytes(b"overlay-image")
        return True, ""

    def fake_run_ffmpeg(args, timeout_seconds=1800):  # type: ignore[no-untyped-def]
        captured["cmds"].append(list(args))
        Path(args[-1]).parent.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
        Path(args[-1]).write_bytes(b"generated")
        return True, ""

    monkeypatch.setattr(stage8_cover, "_render_title_overlay_image", fake_render_overlay)
    monkeypatch.setattr(stage8_cover, "run_ffmpeg", fake_run_ffmpeg)

    result = stage8_cover.run(
        input_video=str(source),
        output_dir=str(out_dir),
        task_id=task_id,
        forum_post_info={"cover_titles": ["up-line", "down-line"]},
    )
    assert result.success

    manifest = stage_dir / f"{task_id}_stage8_cover_manifest.json"
    payload = json.loads(manifest.read_text(encoding="utf-8"))
    assert "video_title_overlay" in str(payload.get("method", ""))
    assert payload.get("overlay_duration_seconds") == 1.7

    overlay_cmd = captured["cmds"][-1]
    filter_expr = overlay_cmd[overlay_cmd.index("-filter_complex") + 1]
    assert "between(t,0,1.700)" in filter_expr


def test_stage8_strict_mode_rejects_when_overlay_not_applied(monkeypatch, tmp_path) -> None:  # type: ignore[no-untyped-def]
    source = tmp_path / "input.mp4"
    source.write_bytes(b"fake-video")
    out_dir = tmp_path / "out"
    task_id = "v8_strict"
    monkeypatch.setenv("CLONECUT_STRICT_EQUIVALENCE", "true")
    monkeypatch.setattr(stage8_cover, "ffmpeg_exists", lambda: False)

    result = stage8_cover.run(
        input_video=str(source),
        output_dir=str(out_dir),
        task_id=task_id,
        forum_post_info={"cover_title_up": "strict-title"},
    )
    assert result.success is False
    assert "strict equivalence" in str(result.error_message)

    stage_dir = out_dir / task_id / "_stages"
    manifest = stage_dir / f"{task_id}_stage8_cover_manifest.json"
    payload = json.loads(manifest.read_text(encoding="utf-8"))
    assert payload.get("strict_rejected") is True
    assert payload.get("fallback_used") is True

Output:
from __future__ import annotations

import json
from pathlib import Path

from domain.video import VideoPipeline, VideoPipelineConfig
from domain.video.stages.base import StageResult


def test_internal_pipeline_skeleton_runs(tmp_path) -> None:  # type: ignore[no-untyped-def]
    source = tmp_path / "input.mp4"
    source.write_bytes(b"fake-video")

    cfg = VideoPipelineConfig(output_dir=str(tmp_path / "out"))
    pipeline = VideoPipeline(cfg)
    result = pipeline.run(input_video=str(source), task_id="video-1")

    assert result.success
    assert result.output_file is not None
    assert Path(result.output_file).exists()
    assert "stage0_clean" in result.executed_stages
    assert "stage1_transcode" in result.executed_stages
    assert "stage2_silence" in result.executed_stages
    assert "stage3_repair" in result.executed_stages
    assert "stage4_asr" in result.executed_stages
    assert "stage5_ai_text" in result.executed_stages
    assert "stage6_clip" in result.executed_stages
    assert "stage6_5_resub" in result.executed_stages
    assert "stage7_correct" in result.executed_stages
    assert "stage7_subtitle" in result.executed_stages
    assert "stage8_cover" in result.executed_stages
    assert "stage9_organize" in result.executed_stages
    assert result.current_stage == 11
    assert result.failed_stage is None
    srt = tmp_path / "out" / "video-1" / "_stages" / "video-1_stage4_asr_subtitles.srt"
    manifest = tmp_path / "out" / "video-1" / "_stages" / "video-1_stage8_cover_manifest.json"
    assert srt.exists()
    assert manifest.exists()


def test_internal_pipeline_failure_exposes_breakpoint(tmp_path) -> None:  # type: ignore[no-untyped-def]
    cfg = VideoPipelineConfig(output_dir=str(tmp_path / "out"))
    pipeline = VideoPipeline(cfg)
    missing = tmp_path / "missing.mp4"

    result = pipeline.run(input_video=str(missing), task_id="video-2", start_from_stage=1)
    assert not result.success
    assert result.failed_stage == "stage1_transcode"
    assert result.current_stage == 1


def test_pipeline_stage4_manifest_contains_forum_context(monkeypatch, tmp_path) -> None:  # type: ignore[no-untyped-def]
    monkeypatch.setenv("CLONECUT_ASR_BACKEND", "sidecar")
    source = tmp_path / "input.mp4"
    source.write_bytes(b"fake-video")
    sidecar = tmp_path / "shared.srt"
    sidecar.write_text(
        "\n".join(
            [
                "1",
                "00:00:00,000 --> 00:00:02,000",
                "pipeline context subtitle",
                "",
            ]
        ),
        encoding="utf-8",
    )
    monkeypatch.setenv("CLONECUT_ASR_SIDECAR_PATH", str(sidecar))

    cfg = VideoPipelineConfig(output_dir=str(tmp_path / "out"))
    pipeline = VideoPipeline(cfg)
    result = pipeline.run(
        input_video=str(source),
        task_id="video-ctx",
        forum_post_info={"hotwords": ["topic-a", "topic-b"], "speaker": "spk-1"},
    )

    assert result.success
    manifest = tmp_path / "out" / "video-ctx" / "_stages" / "video-ctx_stage4_asr_manifest.json"
    payload = json.loads(manifest.read_text(encoding="utf-8"))
    assert payload.get("engine") == "sidecar_srt"
    assert payload.get("hotwords") == ["topic-a", "topic-b"]
    assert payload.get("hotword_count") == 2
    assert payload.get("speaker") == "spk-1"


def test_pipeline_stage4_receives_explicit_hotwords(monkeypatch, tmp_path) -> None:  # type: ignore[no-untyped-def]
    source = tmp_path / "input.mp4"
    source.write_bytes(b"fake-video")
    cfg = VideoPipelineConfig(output_dir=str(tmp_path / "out"))
    pipeline = VideoPipeline(cfg)
    captured: dict = {}

    def fake_stage4(input_video, output_dir, task_id, *, forum_post_info=None, hotwords=None, speaker=None):  # type: ignore[no-untyped-def]
        captured["forum_post_info"] = forum_post_info
        captured["hotwords"] = hotwords
        captured["speaker"] = speaker
        return StageResult(success=True, output_file=input_video)

    monkeypatch.setattr("domain.video.pipeline.run_stage4_asr", fake_stage4)
    result = pipeline._stage4(  # type: ignore[attr-defined]
        str(source),
        task_id="video-stage4-ctx",
        stage_name="stage4_asr",
        context={
            "forum_post_info": {
                "hotwords": ["topic-a", "topic-b", "topic-a"],
                "speaker": "spk-x",
            }
        },
    )

    assert result.success
    assert captured["hotwords"] == ["topic-a", "topic-b"]
    assert captured["speaker"] == "spk-x"


def test_pipeline_stage8_cover_titles_mapping_from_english_keys(monkeypatch, tmp_path) -> None:  # type: ignore[no-untyped-def]
    source = tmp_path / "input.mp4"
    source.write_bytes(b"fake-video")
    cfg = VideoPipelineConfig(output_dir=str(tmp_path / "out"))
    pipeline = VideoPipeline(cfg)
    captured: dict = {}

    def fake_stage8(input_video, output_dir, task_id, *, forum_post_info=None):  # type: ignore[no-untyped-def]
        captured["forum_post_info"] = forum_post_info
        return StageResult(success=True, output_file=input_video)

    monkeypatch.setattr("domain.video.pipeline.run_stage8_cover", fake_stage8)
    result = pipeline._stage8(  # type: ignore[attr-defined]
        str(source),
        task_id="video-stage8-cover",
        stage_name="stage8_cover",
        context={
            "forum_post_info": {
                "cover_title_up": "line-up",
                "cover_title_middle": "line-middle",
                "cover_title_down": "line-down",
            }
        },
    )

    assert result.success
    mapped = captured["forum_post_info"]
    assert mapped["cover_titles"] == ["line-up", "line-middle", "line-down"]
    assert mapped["封面标题上"] == "line-up"
    assert mapped["封面标题中"] == "line-middle"
    assert mapped["封面标题下"] == "line-down"


def test_pipeline_stage5_receives_forum_post_info(monkeypatch, tmp_path) -> None:  # type: ignore[no-untyped-def]
    source = tmp_path / "input.mp4"
    source.write_bytes(b"fake-video")
    cfg = VideoPipelineConfig(output_dir=str(tmp_path / "out"))
    pipeline = VideoPipeline(cfg)
    captured: dict = {}

    def fake_stage5(input_video, output_dir, task_id, *, forum_post_info=None):  # type: ignore[no-untyped-def]
        captured["forum_post_info"] = forum_post_info
        return StageResult(success=True, output_file=input_video)

    monkeypatch.setattr("domain.video.pipeline.run_stage5_ai_text", fake_stage5)
    result = pipeline._stage5(  # type: ignore[attr-defined]
        str(source),
        task_id="video-stage5-ctx",
        stage_name="stage5_ai_text",
        context={
            "forum_post_info": {
                "hotwords": ["hot-a", "hot-b"],
                "speaker": "spk-z",
            }
        },
    )

    assert result.success
    forwarded = captured["forum_post_info"]
    assert forwarded["hotwords"] == ["hot-a", "hot-b"]
    assert forwarded["speaker"] == "spk-z"

 def test_attach_video_render_manifest_collects_primary_subtitle_cover_and_state(monkeypatch, tmp_path) -> None:  # type: ignore[no-untyped-def]
@@
     pipeline_state = render_manifest.get("pipeline_state") or {}
     assert pipeline_state.get("last_completed_step") == 9
 
 
+def test_attach_video_render_manifest_prefers_stage7_subtitles_and_stage8_cover_overlay(
+    monkeypatch, tmp_path
+) -> None:  # type: ignore[no-untyped-def]
+    monkeypatch.setenv("CLONECUT_DB_PATH", str(tmp_path / "clonecut_render_manifest_stage_named.db"))
+    cfg = _build_config(tmp_path)
+    service = WorkerService(cfg)
+
+    task_id = "render-manifest-stage-named-task"
+    task_dir = Path(cfg.output_dir) / task_id
+    task_dir.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
+    stage_dir = task_dir / "_stages"
+    stage_dir.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
+
+    final_video = task_dir / "论坛样本_final.mp4"
+    final_video.write_bytes(b"final-video")
+    (stage_dir / f"{task_id}_stage4_asr_subtitles.srt").write_text(
+        "1\n00:00:00,000 --> 00:00:01,000\nasr subtitle\n",
+        encoding="utf-8",
+    )
+    (stage_dir / f"{task_id}_stage7_correct_subtitles.srt").write_text(
+        "1\n00:00:00,000 --> 00:00:01,000\nfinal subtitle\n",
+        encoding="utf-8",
+    )
+    (stage_dir / f"{task_id}_stage6_5_resub_subtitles.ass").write_text(
+        "[Script Info]\nTitle: stage6_5 ass\n",
+        encoding="utf-8",
+    )
+    (stage_dir / f"{task_id}_stage8_cover_overlay.png").write_bytes(b"overlay")
+    (task_dir / "clip_text.txt").write_text("裁剪文本", encoding="utf-8")
+    state_file = task_dir / "state.json"
+    state_file.write_text(
+        json.dumps(
+            {
+                "last_completed_step": 9,
+                "last_completed_step_name": "stage9_organize",
+                "output_file": str(final_video),
+            },
+            ensure_ascii=False,
+        ),
+        encoding="utf-8",
+    )
+
+    task = UnifiedTask(
+        task_id=task_id,
+        task_type=TaskType.VIDEO,
+        source="unit",
+        source_path=str(tmp_path / "source.mp4"),
+    )
+    Path(str(task.source_path)).write_bytes(b"source-video")
+
+    result = service._attach_video_render_manifest(
+        task,
+        {
+            "success": True,
+            "request_type": "video",
+            "engine": "internal_pipeline",
+            "output_path": str(final_video),
+        },
+    )
+
+    artifacts = result.get("artifacts") or []
+    roles = {str(item.get("role")): item for item in artifacts}
+    assert "subtitle_srt" in roles
+    assert "subtitle_ass" in roles
+    assert "cover_image" in roles
+    assert roles["subtitle_srt"].get("filename") == f"{task_id}_stage7_correct_subtitles.srt"
+    assert roles["subtitle_ass"].get("filename") == f"{task_id}_stage6_5_resub_subtitles.ass"
+    assert roles["cover_image"].get("filename") == f"{task_id}_stage8_cover_overlay.png"
+
+    render_manifest = result.get("render_manifest") or {}
+    subtitles = render_manifest.get("subtitles") or {}
+    assert subtitles.get("srt") == roles["subtitle_srt"].get("artifact_id")
+    assert subtitles.get("ass") == roles["subtitle_ass"].get("artifact_id")
+    cover = render_manifest.get("cover") or {}
+    assert cover.get("image") == roles["cover_image"].get("artifact_id")
+
+
 def test_internal_pipeline_context_keeps_only_forum_snapshot_and_clip_text(monkeypatch, tmp_path) -> None:  # type: ignore[no-untyped-def]
     monkeypatch.setenv("CLONECUT_DB_PATH", str(tmp_path / "clonecut_internal_context.db"))
     cfg = _build_config(tmp_path)
     service = WorkerService(cfg)

+def test_stage8_fallback_also_writes_cover_overlay_image(monkeypatch, tmp_path) -> None:  # type: ignore[no-untyped-def]
+    source = tmp_path / "input.mp4"
+    source.write_bytes(b"fake-video")
+    out_dir = tmp_path / "out"
+    task_id = "v8_fallback_overlay"
+
+    monkeypatch.setattr(stage8_cover, "ffmpeg_exists", lambda: False)
+    result = stage8_cover.run(
+        input_video=str(source),
+        output_dir=str(out_dir),
+        task_id=task_id,
+        forum_post_info={"cover_title_up": "主标题"},
+    )
+    assert result.success
+
+    stage_dir = out_dir / task_id / "_stages"
+    overlay = stage_dir / f"{task_id}_stage8_cover_overlay.png"
+    assert overlay.exists()
+    assert overlay.read_bytes()
+
+
 def test_stage8_applies_video_title_overlay_with_subtitle_timing(monkeypatch, tmp_path) -> None:  # type: ignore[no-untyped-def]
     source = tmp_path / "input.mp4"
     source.write_bytes(b"fake-video")

tmp_path = WindowsPath('C:/Users/ai/AppData/Local/Temp/pytest-of-ai/pytest-150/test_attach_video_render_manif0')

    def test_attach_video_render_manifest_prefers_stage7_subtitles_and_stage8_cover_overlay(
        monkeypatch, tmp_path
    ) -> None:  # type: ignore[no-untyped-def]
        monkeypatch.setenv("CLONECUT_DB_PATH", str(tmp_path / "clonecut_render_manifest_stage_named.db"))
        cfg = _build_config(tmp_path)
        service = WorkerService(cfg)
    
        task_id = "render-manifest-stage-named-task"
        task_dir = Path(cfg.output_dir) / task_id
        task_dir.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
        stage_dir = task_dir / "_stages"
        stage_dir.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
    
        final_video = task_dir / "论坛样本_final.mp4"
        final_video.write_bytes(b"final-video")
        (stage_dir / f"{task_id}_stage4_asr_subtitles.srt").write_text(
            "1\n00:00:00,000 --> 00:00:01,000\nasr subtitle\n",
            encoding="utf-8",
        )
        (stage_dir / f"{task_id}_stage7_correct_subtitles.srt").write_text(
            "1\n00:00:00,000 --> 00:00:01,000\nfinal subtitle\n",
            encoding="utf-8",
        )
        (stage_dir / f"{task_id}_stage6_5_resub_subtitles.ass").write_text(
            "[Script Info]\nTitle: stage6_5 ass\n",
            encoding="utf-8",
        )
        (stage_dir / f"{task_id}_stage8_cover_overlay.png").write_bytes(b"overlay")
        (task_dir / "clip_text.txt").write_text("裁剪文本", encoding="utf-8")
        state_file = task_dir / "state.json"
        state_file.write_text(
            json.dumps(
                {
                    "last_completed_step": 9,
                    "last_completed_step_name": "stage9_organize",
                    "output_file": str(final_video),
                },
                ensure_ascii=False,
            ),
            encoding="utf-8",
        )
    
        task = UnifiedTask(
            task_id=task_id,
            task_type=TaskType.VIDEO,
            source="unit",
            source_path=str(tmp_path / "source.mp4"),
        )
        Path(str(task.source_path)).write_bytes(b"source-video")
    
        result = service._attach_video_render_manifest(
            task,
            {
                "success": True,
                "request_type": "video",
                "engine": "internal_pipeline",
                "output_path": str(final_video),
            },

        artifacts = result.get("artifacts") or []
